behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: This can fail at the real git boundary (rename detection/status semantics) even if unit parsing logic passes. The behavior is externally visible CLI output over a real repository state, so a subprocess round-trip check is needed. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because the behavior text is git/report logic only, with no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, then run the CLI as a real subprocess against that repo. Assert stdout report shows the file only in `renamed`, and `added`/`deleted` counts do not include that rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling often fails only with real subprocess output/encoding and real git behavior; mocks can hide crashes. This needs real CLI execution and real repo content to avoid false confidence. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model output quality or multi-agent chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, add/modify a real binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI subprocess, and assert exit code is success, no crash, and binary file contributes to category counts in stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This is a real external error-path contract with git (unborn HEAD/no commits) that unit tests with stubs commonly miss. System triad error-path trigger applies directly. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text contains no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a brand-new git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert successful exit plus an empty/zeroed structured report on stdout instead of an error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires proving externally visible correctness via round-trip readback from a real repository history to CLI stdout counts. In-process fakes can agree with themselves and miss git/history edge behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this is deterministic VCS/data processing, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run the CLI subprocess, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from real git history queries.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are governed by real git index/working-tree behavior; mocked module tests can miss boundary mismatches. Verifying identical externally visible CLI output across both states requires real repo + subprocess execution. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML output dependency or multi-agent sequencing is described.
behaviors.test_description: Using the same file changes in a temp repo, run the CLI once with changes unstaged and once after staging them, then assert the structured stdout reports are identical (or semantically equivalent if ordering differs).
